gameInfo.title: Pokémon Pokopia
gameInfo.description: A life simulation game where you play as a Ditto to help an island become a Pokémon Paradise. Players mimic Pokémon abilities to develop island areas and construct a town with Professor Tangrowth's guidance. Set in ruined areas of Kanto long after the events of Pokémon HeartGold & SoulSilver.

uniquePokemon.name: Professor Tangrowth
uniquePokemon.description: A Tangrowth variant who serves as the player's guide. Has been living alone in a place where humans and Pokémon once lived.
uniquePokemon.role: Assists Ditto in rebuilding an abandoned area into a thriving habitat for Pokémon. Has the Appraise specialty.
uniquePokemon.howToObtain: Encountered as part of the main story narrative

uniquePokemon.name: Peakychu
uniquePokemon.description: An unusual pale-colored Pikachu variant with droopier ears than the standard form.
uniquePokemon.role: Has the unique Illuminate specialty. Key role in the Bleak Beach story quest - creates seashell lamps and helps light up the area.

uniquePokemon.name: Mosslax
uniquePokemon.description: A Snorlax variant which has had moss grow on its body and a flower on its head, as if it has been asleep for a very long time.
uniquePokemon.role: Associated with the Mosslax Boosts mechanic. Has the Eat specialty. Awakened by Raikou's thunder in the Bleak Beach story quest.
uniquePokemon.howToObtain: Discovered behind a wall in Bleak Beach during the Brighten Things Up quest

uniquePokemon.name: Smearguru
uniquePokemon.description: A renowned painter Smeargle variant with paint colors covering its body.
uniquePokemon.role: Artistic character with painting-related significance. Has the Paint specialty.

uniquePokemon.name: Stereo Rotom
uniquePokemon.description: A Rotom variant that serves as a DJ.
uniquePokemon.role: Has the unique DJ specialty. Used in the Rocky Ridges party quest.

uniquePokemon.name: Chef Dente
uniquePokemon.role: Provides food items (Bruised Berry, Common Candy, Rare Candy). Important for party quest and building quest in story progression.

legendaryPokemon.name: Kyogre
legendaryPokemon.role: Arrives and produces lasting rainfall after Slowpoke yawns during the Withered Wastelands quest (Yawn Up A Storm).
legendaryPokemon.encounter: Story event in Withered Wastelands after achieving 100% humidity

legendaryPokemon.name: Raikou
legendaryPokemon.role: Its thunder awakens Mosslax during the Bleak Beach quest (Brighten Things Up).
legendaryPokemon.encounter: Story event in Bleak Beach; also encountered on Dream Islands

legendaryPokemon.name: Entei
legendaryPokemon.encounter: Can be encountered on Dream Islands

legendaryPokemon.name: Suicune
legendaryPokemon.encounter: Can be encountered on Dream Islands

legendaryPokemon.name: Volcanion
legendaryPokemon.role: Appears and sets off fireworks after completing the Rocky Ridges party quest (Time To Party).
legendaryPokemon.encounter: Story event in Rocky Ridges after party celebration

legendaryPokemon.name: Mewtwo
legendaryPokemon.role: Appears impressed at building completion during the Sparkling Skylands quest (Rebuild the Huge Building). A portal opens on the roof.
legendaryPokemon.encounter: Story event in Sparkling Skylands; also encountered on Dream Islands

legendaryPokemon.name: Articuno
legendaryPokemon.encounter: Referenced in Clear Bell/Tidal Bell crafting requirements

legendaryPokemon.name: Zapdos
legendaryPokemon.encounter: Referenced in Clear Bell/Tidal Bell crafting requirements

legendaryPokemon.name: Moltres
legendaryPokemon.encounter: Referenced in Clear Bell/Tidal Bell crafting requirements

regions.name: Withered Wastelands
regions.description: A ruined area of Kanto that needs rainfall restored. The first major area players work to restore.
regions.mainQuest: Yawn Up A Storm - Restore rainfall by working with Slowpoke to get Kyogre to arrive
features: Rocket location where Magnet Rise is learned
Slowpoke habitat needed for main quest

regions.name: Bleak Beach
regions.description: A perpetually dark coastal area that needs to be illuminated. Features the S.S. Anne wreck.
regions.mainQuest: Brighten Things Up - Generate power and illuminate the area with Peakychu to awaken Mosslax via Raikou's thunder

regions.name: Rocky Ridges
regions.description: A mountainous area with caves and hot springs. Needs community spirit restored through celebration.
regions.mainQuest: Time To Party - Organize a celebration to attract Pokémon and summon Volcanion

regions.name: Sparkling Skylands
regions.description: A high-altitude area in the sky with a massive damaged building that needs reconstruction.
regions.mainQuest: Rebuild the Huge Building - Reconstruct a massive structure with Tinkaton's guidance to summon Mewtwo

regions.name: Palette Town
regions.description: A town area that can be developed. Features shops and trading facilities.

regions.name: Cloud Island
regions.description: A special sky island accessible for additional gameplay.

habitats.name: Tall Grass
habitats.description: Four tufts of tall grass bunched together in a plot. The perfect hiding place for small Pokémon.

habitats.name: Chilly Shaved Ice
habitats.description: Shaved ice served with frozen toppings.

habitats.name: Lovely Ribbon Cake
habitats.description: The large ribbon and heart decorations make this cake look quite lovely.

eventHabitats.name: Yellow Carpet
eventHabitats.description: A field of warm, blooming yellow flowers filling with energy.

eventHabitats.name: Field-trip Friends
eventHabitats.description: Pack a lunch box and canteen inside your backpack.

eventHabitats.name: Dandelion Lunchtime
eventHabitats.description: Lunch box? Check. Tableware? Check. For a picnic lunch.

specialties.name: Grow
specialties.description: Plant and grow vegetation. Used for farming berries and crops.

specialties.name: Burn
specialties.description: Use fire abilities. Set fires, power furnaces, and cook.

specialties.name: Water
specialties.description: Use water abilities like Water Gun. Increase water levels and create rivers.

specialties.name: Fly
specialties.description: Fly between areas and reach elevated locations.

specialties.name: Search
specialties.description: Find hidden items and materials in the environment.

specialties.name: Chop
specialties.description: Cut down trees and chop wood for lumber.

specialties.name: Litter
specialties.description: Scatter materials like Fluff. Some Pokémon produce crafting materials.

specialties.name: Trade
specialties.description: Trade items with other Pokémon and NPCs.

specialties.name: Generate
specialties.description: Generate electricity to power things like stations and machines.

specialties.name: Recycle
specialties.description: Recycle wastepaper into paper and process garbage into materials.

specialties.name: Crush
specialties.description: Crush rocks and boulders. Used with cement mixer to make Concrete.

specialties.name: Bulldoze
specialties.description: Flatten terrain and clear land for construction.

specialties.name: Build
specialties.description: Construct buildings, bridges, and structures.

specialties.name: Hype
specialties.description: Boost morale and mood. Used to increase party atmosphere.

specialties.name: Gather
specialties.description: Collect scattered materials efficiently.

specialties.name: Gather Honey
specialties.description: Unique to Vespiquen. Collect honey from flower patches.

specialties.name: Storage
specialties.description: Store and manage items. Associated with Gulpin/Swalot.

specialties.name: Teleport
specialties.description: Teleport between locations.

specialties.name: Yawn
specialties.description: Unique to Slowpoke. Used in the Withered Wastelands quest to trigger rainfall.

specialties.name: Dream Island
specialties.description: Unique to Drifloon. Enables access to Dream Islands once per day.

specialties.name: Explode
specialties.description: Associated with Voltorb/Electrode. Explosive abilities.

specialties.name: Paint
specialties.description: Paint and create art. Associated with Smeargle/Smearguru.

specialties.name: Illuminate
specialties.description: Unique to Peakychu. Light up dark areas.

specialties.name: DJ
specialties.description: Unique to Stereo Rotom. Play music for events and parties.

specialties.name: Appraise
specialties.description: Unique to Professor Tangrowth. Evaluate and study items and relics.

specialties.name: Eat
specialties.description: Unique to Mosslax. Consume large amounts.

specialties.name: Transform
specialties.description: Unique to Ditto (player character). Mimic other Pokémon's abilities.
Unknown specialty. Associated with Magikarp.

materials.name: Honey
materials.description: Sweet-smelling honey. If you spread some where a Pokémon lives, that Pokémon will return home.

materials.name: Sturdy Stick
materials.description: A branch that fell off a tree somewhere. Perfect for making various toys and everyday items.

materials.name: Stone
materials.description: Just a normal stone you can find anywhere. It can be used as a material for a lot of things.

materials.name: Leaf
materials.description: A lush, vivid green leaf that you can obtain by cutting trees and grass.

materials.name: Small Log
materials.description: These can be processed into lumber, but they can also be used as is for certain crafting projects.

materials.name: Vine Rope
materials.description: Obtained by cutting vines, which grow in caves and high-altitude areas.

materials.name: Glowing Mushrooms
materials.description: Bioluminescent mushrooms. You can't eat them, but they'd probably make great lights!

materials.name: Twine
materials.description: Obtained by destroying textile garbage. Bug-type Pokémon can also produce it.
materials.source: Bleak Beach, Sparkling Skylands, destroy fabric

materials.name: Sea Glass Fragments
materials.description: Pieces of glass that have been polished and buffed by ocean waves. They're used to make glass objects.

materials.name: Seashell
materials.description: A common seashell found on sandy beaches. This is useful material for building houses.

materials.name: Nonburnable Garbage
materials.description: Trash discarded here and there. Apparently some Pokémon are big fans of garbage.

materials.name: Squishy Clay
materials.description: A soft malleable crafting material used for all sorts of things. It can also be fired and hardened into bricks.

materials.name: Fluff
materials.description: An irresistibly soft tuft of fluff. Pokémon with the Litter specialty may scatter this.

materials.name: Wastepaper
materials.description: These look tough to craft in their current state. Try recycling them into regular paper.

materials.name: Copper Ore
materials.description: Raw mineral copper. When processed into ingots, it can be used as a crafting material.

materials.name: Iron Ore
materials.description: Metal that can be obtained from iron deposits. It can also be found among metal debris.
materials.source: Rocky Ridges, Sparkling Skylands, Palette Town

materials.name: Gold Ore
materials.description: Metal extracted from gold deposits. It glitters with a luxurious sheen.

materials.name: Pokémetal Fragment
materials.description: Colorful, sparkling metal extracted from Pokémetal deposits.

materials.name: Rare Pokémetal Fragment
materials.description: This can sometimes be found in Pokémetal deposits. If you find one, consider yourself extremely lucky!

materials.name: Crystal Fragment
materials.description: A piece of broken crystal. It's transparent, so you can see right through it.

materials.name: Lumber
materials.description: Wood that's been cut so it's easy to use. If you're making something out of wood, this is for you.

materials.name: Brick
materials.description: Bricks made by baking clay. They're perfect for making buildings, roads and other structures.

materials.name: Copper Ingot
materials.description: Copper that has been processed in a smelting furnace. It kinda looks like chocolate...

materials.name: Iron Ingot
materials.description: Iron that has been processed in a smelting furnace. It's really hard and super heavy.

materials.name: Gold Ingot
materials.description: Gold that has been processed in a smelting furnace. Just looking at it makes you feel fancy.

materials.name: Pokémetal
materials.description: Made from smelted Pokémetal fragments, these can be used to make various items.

materials.name: Rare Pokémetal
materials.description: Made from smelted rare Pokémetal fragments, these can be used to make various items.

materials.name: Glass
materials.description: You can make glass by melting sand or sandstone in a furnace, or obtain it by smashing broken windows.

materials.name: Concrete
materials.description: Made from a thick liquid that has hardened. This stuff is solid and super sturdy.

materials.name: Paper
materials.description: Pristine white paper made from recycled wastepaper.

materials.name: Tinkagear
materials.description: A cool gear produced by Tinkaton. You can sense their dedication to the craft contained within it.

materials.name: Strange Strings
materials.description: These strings make a cool sound when plucked. Use them to make guitars and bass guitars.

materials.name: Armor Fragment
materials.description: A piece of shattered armor. Gather all the shards to create armor brimming with emotions.

materials.name: Stardust
materials.description: Sand that can be processed into Star Pieces. It can be found on Dream Islands.

materials.name: Beach Sand
materials.description: Sand found on beaches.

materials.name: Limestone
materials.description: A sedimentary rock used for luxury crafting.

materials.name: Glowing Stone
materials.description: A stone that emits a faint glow.

materials.name: Wildflowers
materials.description: Fresh wildflowers used for decoration.

materials.name: Rainbow Feather
materials.description: A rare feather required for crafting the Clear Bell.

materials.name: Silver Feather
materials.description: A rare feather required for crafting the Tidal Bell.

quests.name: Yawn Up A Storm
quests.description: Restore rainfall to the Withered Wastelands by working with Slowpoke.
objectives: Set up habitat for Slowpoke to appear
Use Water Gun to increase water levels
Break rocks to create rivers
Create flower patches to boost humidity
Achieve 100% humidity
Get Slowpoke to yawn
Kyogre arrives and produces lasting rainfall
quests.rewards: Increases Trainer Rank and environment restoration. Kyogre permanently restores rainfall.

quests.name: Do the Team Initiation Challenge
quests.description: A strange building on the coast of the Withered Wastelands presents tasks to complete.
objectives: Complete nine different challenges
Bring various items found throughout progression
Collect badges with each completion

quests.name: Brighten Things Up
quests.description: Combat perpetual darkness in Bleak Beach by generating power and illuminating the area.
objectives: Help Meowth with tasks using Pawmi's electricity
Break wall to discover Mosslax
Consult Professor Tangrowth
Light up the area incrementally
Make flowers bloom on Mosslax
Create seashell lamps in cave with Peakychu
Connect power stations and activate furnaces/water wheels
Generate sufficient power for Peakychu to use Illuminate
Raikou's thunder awakens Mosslax

quests.name: Time To Party
quests.description: Build community spirit in Rocky Ridges by organizing a celebration to attract Pokémon.
objectives: Obtain Party Pot
Find a CD for DJ Rotom
Locate and appraise Lost Relic (Large)
Create habitat for Ludicolo (Hype ability Pokémon)
Craft 5 Party Platters with varied meals
Increase mood to 100 using decorations and Pokémon
Prepare celebration feast (15 Leppa Berry, 15 Wheat, 15 Beans, 5 Honey)
Bring 5 Pokémon including Chef Dente
quests.rewards: Volcanion appears and sets off fireworks

quests.name: Rebuild the Huge Building
quests.description: Reconstruct a massive damaged structure in Sparkling Skylands with Tinkaton's guidance.
objectives: Obtain concrete from Conkeldurr
Activate lifts using Tinkagears
Build 2F: 25 Concrete, 10 Glass, 5 Pokémetal, 10 Iron Ingots + 6 Pokémon (including Professor Tangrowth)
Build 3F: 25 Concrete, 10 Glass, 10 Glowing Stone, 15 Copper Ingots + 10 Pokémon (Chef Dente recommended)
Build 4F: 40 Concrete, 10 Glass, 10 Paper, 10 Brick, 10 Lumber + 15 Pokémon (Peakychu recommended)
quests.rewards: Portal opens on roof; Mewtwo appears impressed at building completion

dreamIslands.name: Pikachu Island
dreamIslands.description: A Dream Island themed after Pikachu.

dreamIslands.name: Eevee Island
dreamIslands.description: A Dream Island themed after Eevee.

dreamIslands.name: Clefairy Island
dreamIslands.description: A Dream Island themed after Clefairy.

dreamIslands.name: Arcanine Island
dreamIslands.description: A Dream Island themed after Arcanine.

dreamIslands.name: Dragonite Island
dreamIslands.description: A Dream Island themed after Dragonite.

dreamIslands.name: Ditto Island
dreamIslands.description: A Dream Island themed after Ditto with random material drops.

dreamIslands.name: Substitute Island
dreamIslands.description: A Dream Island themed after Substitute with random material drops.

dreamIslandInfo.description: Dream Islands are special locations accessible once per day after befriending Drifloon. They provide rare items and ore such as gold and Poké Metal.
features: No wild Pokémon present on islands
Resources reset daily; uncollected items cannot be retrieved
Potential legendary encounters: Raikou, Entei, Suicune, and Mewtwo
Rare finds include character-themed outfits (Blue, Green, Ethan) and notebooks with lore

magnetRise.description: A transformation learned from Magnemite after completing the story. Found beneath a rocket in the Withered Wastelands.
magnetRise.buildFeature: Place up to 9 blocks simultaneously (vs 1 in human form). Allows vertical and horizontal rotation of block placements. Replace mode to swap existing blocks.
magnetRise.breakFeature: Press Y on items to break them down for resources. Plants yield seeds; worn lumber converts to usable lumber. Consumes less PP than Rock Smash or Cut. Hold and charge for full item collection of previously unpickable materials.
magnetRise.note: One of the most important abilities in the game due to its overall utility across exploration and building mechanics.

friendship.description: Befriend Pokémon by completing their requests, giving gifts, or playing games with them.
friendship.progression: Friendship gauge increases through interactions. At max friendship, Pokémon announce they are your best friend.
rewards: Pokémon display happiness upon seeing you
Pokémon give found materials as gifts
Pokémon address you by your given name instead of Ditto
Special mark on Pokédex entry indicating Best Friend status

crafting.description: Craft items at workbenches using gathered materials. Items can be produced in multiples.

cooking.description: Cook meals that power up Ditto's moves when eaten.

beverages.description: Drinks from vending machines create different environmental effects when spit out.

gameplay.stampCards: Collectible stamp cards for completing activities
gameplay.comfyLevel: Environment & Comfy Level mechanics affect Pokémon satisfaction

cookingRecipes.name: Potato Hamburger Steak
cookingRecipes.effect: Powers up Rock Smash a lot

cookingRecipes.name: Vibrant Hamburger Steak
cookingRecipes.effect: Powers up Rock Smash a lot

craftingRecipes.name: Printer
craftingRecipes.unlock: Complete Pokédex (Hidden Room with Neo Dowsing Machine)

dataSources.note: Data fetched from Serebii.net. Namu.wiki pages returned 403 errors. The Pokémon list may be incomplete beyond #200 due to page content truncation by the fetcher. The game contains approximately 190+ Pokémon spanning multiple generations.
